notes: Principles packets should stay accessible and foundational while still sounding formal and competition-like.
Situations should focus on a single clear decision and use Tier 1 business-administration concepts.
Case prompts should feel analytical, risk-aware, and centered on legal or ethical business decisions.
Even in solo practice mode, the participant packet should frame the student as presenting a clear recommendation.
Participant packet tone should feel formal, direct, and competition-like.
Event situations often place the participant in an HR role speaking to a director, president, or owner.
Scenarios frequently include company size, staffing context, and a specific people-management challenge.
Scenarios should center on a practical management decision with realistic tradeoffs.
Use a single decision-maker judge such as an owner or operations manager.
Principles of Marketing packets should stay foundational and accessible while still sounding competition-ready.
Scenarios should focus on one clear customer or promotion decision with practical business logic.
Apparel and accessories packets should emphasize merchandising, assortment, presentation, and customer appeal.
Automotive marketing scenarios should balance trust, service credibility, and realistic local promotion strategy.
Marketing scenarios should mention audience, positioning, or promotion goals.
Keep the business context realistic for a district or state DECA roleplay.
Buying and merchandising cases should focus on product decisions, customer demand, and assortment strategy.
Food marketing scenarios should sound fast-moving, customer-aware, and easy to visualize in a real local market.
Integrated event campaigns should feel coordinated across channels and tied to one clear objective.
Integrated product campaigns should connect product positioning, audience, and promotional consistency.
Integrated service campaigns should emphasize trust, value communication, and consistent message delivery.
Marketing communications packets should highlight message clarity, channel choice, and audience response.
Marketing management team cases should feel strategic, customer-focused, and measurable.
Professional selling packets should make the participant sound consultative, confident, and focused on customer fit.
Retail merchandising scenarios should center on assortment, display, traffic, and product performance.
Sports and entertainment marketing scenarios should feel audience-driven, sponsor-aware, and promotional.
Sports and entertainment team cases should focus on fan response, promotional execution, and measurable outcomes.
Principles of Finance packets should stay accessible and concept-driven while still sounding formal and DECA-like.
Scenarios should focus on one clear finance decision with foundational business logic.
Accounting roleplays should emphasize accuracy, controls, reporting logic, and practical financial recordkeeping.
The participant should sound organized, precise, and comfortable explaining why the accounting recommendation matters.
Business finance scenarios should feel managerial and decision-oriented, not just technical.
The participant should connect the recommendation to business impact, risk, and measurable results.
Finance scenarios should sound grounded, advisory, and risk-aware rather than overly technical.
The participant should recommend a clear action and justify the business logic.
Principles of Hospitality and Tourism packets should stay accessible and guest-focused while still sounding competition-ready.
Scenarios should focus on one clear hospitality or tourism decision with practical business logic.
Hospitality selling scenarios should emphasize guest needs, value, confidence, and practical service outcomes.
Hospitality team cases should center on service strategy, guest experience, and realistic execution tradeoffs.
Hotel and lodging scenarios should focus on occupancy, guest recovery, room operations, and property-level decision making.
Restaurant and food service management packets should balance staffing, consistency, speed, and guest satisfaction.
Travel and tourism cases should feel guest-centered, experience-driven, and tied to realistic itinerary or destination choices.
Restaurant management scenarios should balance staffing, operations, and customer satisfaction.
